market_context: S&P 500 futures rebounded strongly on March 9 from an intraday low of 6584.5 to near 6800, driven by easing Middle East tensions and retreating oil prices below $90 after peaking above $119.[1][3] Upcoming US February inflation data could influence Fed rate cut expectations ahead of the March 17-18 FOMC, with only 4.7% cut probability currently priced in; grain markets pulled back alongside oil.[2][3]

predictions.reasoning: S&P 500 futures rebounded from 6584.5 low to 6800 on March 9 amid de-escalating Middle East tensions and oil drop below $90, maintaining the 6700-7000 range intact.[1] Easing geopolitical risks and potential softer inflation print ahead of FOMC support risk assets.[2][3]

Nasdaq-100 futures climbed over 1% on March 9 alongside S&P rebound from early selling pressure.[1] Reduced oil prices from $119+ peak and war end hopes boost tech sentiment ahead of inflation data.[3][2]

Crude oil retreated below $90 after surging over $119 on Iran tensions, with Trump signaling short-lived conflict and G7 discussing oil reserve releases.[3] Brent at $82-84 with $4-10 risk premium suggests further downside on de-escalation.[2]

Broad equity index futures including Dow showed resilience with S&P rebound to 6800 on March 9 amid oil pullback and easing geopolitics.[1][3] Lower energy prices reduce inflationary pressures ahead of key CPI data.[2]
